data.title: Chevron's Leviathan field offshore Israel ordered to shut down as precaution
data.description:
Chevron (NYSE:CVX) closed +0.6% on Friday, lagging nearly all oil and gas producing peers, after Israel ordered it to shut down production at its huge Leviathan gas field in the eastern Mediterranean Sea, although output from the Tamar field continues to flow.

Chevron (NYSE:CVX), Leviathan's operator and owner of a ~40% stake in the project, confirmed the shutdown after Israel's Energy Ministry issued the order, citing escalating security threats as regional tensions with Iran erupted into open conflict overnight; Energean (OTCPK:EERGF) also suspended output in Israel.

The move cuts off a critical gas supply line to Egypt, which coincides with rising demand due to summer heat and a widening domestic gas shortfall; a prolonged period of lower Israeli supply may force the Egyptian government to bring forward purchases of liquefied natural gas, further tightening global markets.

Gas prices in Europe rose as much 6.6% on Friday.

The Leviathan field, which supplies gas to domestic Israeli customers as well as Jordan and Egypt, is Israel's largest energy asset with 22.9T cf of recoverable gas and responsible for more than 40% of the country's gas production; it had been exporting record volumes to Egypt.

data.title: First Tellurium announces interim CFO appointment
data.description:
• First Tellurium (OTCQB:FSTTF) announced that, effective June 16, 2025, the company’has approved the appointment of president and chief executive officer Tyrone Docherty as interim chief financial officer while former CFO Pamela Saulnier is on medical leave. 
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Franklin Electric buys 1.2M shares from Schaefer Trust, expands buyback program
data.description:
• Franklin Electric (NASDAQ:FELE) acquired 1.2M shares from the Patricia Schaefer Settlement Trust.
  • The purchase price was $86.78/share.
  • The board increased the share repurchase authorization by 1.2M shares.
  • After the transaction, the company remains authorized to repurchase up to 1.13M shares.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: SA Asks: What's the best telecom stock right now?
data.description:
What's the best telecommunications stock right now for investors?

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Gen Alpha and The Value Pendulum for their picks.

Gen Alpha: Verizon (NYSE:VZ) is my top pick in the telecom space right now. It trades at a far lower P/E valuation than peers T-Mobile (NASDAQ:TMUS) and AT&T (NYSE:T). Meanwhile, its operating revenue, operating cash flow, and free cash flow were all up during Q1. VZ's (NYSE:VZ) pending acquisition of Frontier Communications (NASDAQ:FYBR) greatly boosts its fiber footprint, making it a close number 2 to that of AT&T (NYSE:T). With a starting dividend yield of over 6%, investors are well-compensated for holding this telecom giant.

The Value Pendulum: Telekomunikasi Indonesia (NYSE:TLK) has a new $180M buyback plan that commences in late May and lasts for a year. Its guided dividend payout for FY25 was generous at 80%, which beat consensus by six percentage points. Capital returns are the most important investment criterion for telco-focused investors, and TLK has impressed in this area.

  • Top Integrated Telecommunication Services Stocks
  • Top Wireless Telecommunication Services Stocks
  • Top Cable and Satellite Stocks

data.title: Johnson Controls approves $9B share buyback program
data.description:
• Johnson Controls' (NYSE:JCI) board has approved a new $9B share repurchase authorization.
  • This adds to the $1.1B remaining from the 2021 buyback program.
  • Total repurchase capacity now stands at $10.1B.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Alector appoints Neil Berkley as interim CFO
data.description:
• Alector (NASDAQ:ALEC) announced on Friday that Marc Grasso, chief financial officer (CFO) of the company, will conclude his tenure effective June 20, 2025, to pursue other opportunities.
  • Neil Berkley, the company’s chief business officer, will assume the role of interim CFO while continuing in his current position, said the company. 
  • Neil Berkley joined Alector in March 2024 as chief business officer. Before joining Alector, he served as chief corporate development officer at Juvena Therapeutics, chief business officer at AbCellera, and vice president and head of business development at Halozyme Therapeutics. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Shell shares marked seven-session winning streak
data.description:
Shell (NYSE:SHEL) shares clocked seven straight sessions of gains, as the stock moved 1% higher at $72.18 on Friday.

The oil and gas major gained nearly 6% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has advanced 14% so far this year, compared to a 2.4% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

SHEL stock is up 7% over the past one month.

"Shell's management is more cautious, focusing on portfolio housekeeping and risk management rather than aggressive growth or bold bets," pointed out a Seeking Alpha analysis.

Shell will add as much as 12M metric tons of additional liquefied natural gas capacity by the end of the decade from projects under construction, according to a recent report.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, SHEL has a Hold rating with a score of 3.4 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, B+ momentum, while it got a D+ growth factor. 

Turning to the Wall Street community, 15 analysts gave SHEL a Buy and above rating. Two analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while none recommended Sell or lower.

 
 
 
 
 

Seeking Alpha analysts are positive and see the stock as a Buy.

data.title: New Fortress Energy plunges as creditors reportedly organize amid sliding bond prices
data.description:
New Fortress Energy (NASDAQ:NFE) -7.6% in Friday's trading as Bloomberg reports various creditor groups have have formed amid a mounting debt crisis at the liquefied natural gas company.

Some term loan lenders have organized with legal counsel Akin Gump Strauss Hauer & Feld, while a group of creditors led by Capital Research Group is getting advice from Paul Weiss Rifkind Wharton & Garrison, according to the report, which notes that the investors have holdings across the New Fortress (NASDAQ:NFE) debt structure.

The moves come as New Fortress (NFE) has struggled with delayed projects that have slowed its cash flow, and it is late in filing its Q1 report with the SEC, citing in part a delay in the $1B sale of Jamaican operations that was completed last month.

Some New Fortress (NFE) bonds have plunged to record lows below $0.50 on the dollar this month, Bloomberg reported, in the wake of recent downgrades by S&P Global Ratings and Fitch Ratings, with both citing the company's ability to refinance debt.

Meanwhile, doubts are rising about when operations will begin at New Fortress' (NFE) $1.1B LNG export project in northeastern Mexico, which is seen as crucial to the long-term future of the company.

With nearly $9B in debt, negative free cash flow and significantly lower operating profits than expected, New Fortress (NFE) may have to delay the start of the so-called FLNG-2 project, the credit ratings agencies said.
